message:
feat(hostcall,extensions): add io_uring dispatch lane policy, ext workload harness, and OCO heterogeneous test

Introduces three interconnected subsystems for extension dispatch
performance analysis and correctness validation:

io_uring dispatch lane policy (src/hostcall_io_uring_lane.rs):
- Deterministic policy-only module for routing hostcalls to Fast,
  IoUring, or Compat lanes based on IO hints, latency signals, and
  ring saturation — no syscalls, pure decision logic
- IoUringLanePolicy with configurable ring saturation thresholds,
  latency cutoffs, and automatic fallback to Compat when io_uring
  detection fails or ring pressure exceeds capacity

Extension workload harness (src/bin/ext_workloads.rs):
- Comprehensive benchmark binary for measuring extension dispatch
  performance across legacy JS vs Rust-native paths
- Generates hotspot matrices, VoI scheduler reports, and hostcall
  bridge trace events in machine-readable schemas
- Runs without OS threads (block_on only) for CI/sandbox compatibility

OCO heterogeneous test (tests/extensions_oco_heterogeneous.rs):
- End-to-end evidence test comparing static budget control vs
  OCO-adaptive control across multiple workload regimes
- Validates P99 latency regression, error rate delta, and throughput
  ratio thresholds

Also:
- Register hostcall_io_uring_lane module in lib.rs
- Update extension test configs to explicitly disable RegimeShift,
  SafetyEnvelope, and OcoTuner for isolation
- Remove wasm-host from default Cargo features to reduce default
  build dependency surface

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
